what_it_does: This file is a sent noticeboard message in the dragnet-development channel that announces the completion of an auto-reload-on-deploy feature and tells the user to reload the current page once to enable it.
observations: YAML frontmatter defines a noticeboard item with id, board:// address, type 'message', source 'claude_code', state 'sent', and a history transition from null to sent.
The body is a user-facing announcement about auto-reload-on-deploy being active.
It notes the reload will wait until the user is not mid-typing.
It explicitly says the current page is the old version and requires one manual reload now.
Created and updated timestamps are identical, indicating an initial filing with no later edits.
standouts: Auto-reload-on-deploy feature is announced as live.
The update mechanism avoids interrupting the user while typing.
The current page must be manually reloaded once to bootstrap the new behavior.
conventions: Markdown file with YAML frontmatter metadata.
Uses a structured noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, state, author_session, channel, timestamps, and history.
Timestamps are ISO 8601 with timezone offset.
History is an array of state-change records.
concerns: The title field is truncated mid-sentence ('Done — auto-reload-on-deploy is in. From now, whenever'), which may be a filing artifact.
notes: A system-filed channel message announcing a new self-updating deployment behavior and a one-time user reload step.
questions: What application or project is being deployed?
What is 'dragnet-development' and who is the intended audience?
What technical mechanism implements the auto-reload and typing-idle detection?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a sent noticeboard item whose content announces the feature and instructs a one-time reload.
intention_signals: Inform the user that deployed changes will now appear automatically without manual reloads, while prompting a single manual reload to activate the behavior on the current page.
novelty: It treats a deployment UX instruction as a persisted, attributed noticeboard item with state history rather than an ephemeral chat message.
contribution: A timestamped, attributed record of a feature rollout and the required user onboarding action, preserved in the channel memory ledger.
summary_for_embedding: A sent noticeboard item in dragnet-development announcing that auto-reload-on-deploy is active, that updates wait for typing idle, and that the current page needs one manual reload to enable the behavior.
intention_for_embedding: To notify users of a new self-updating deployment feature and instruct them to reload once to bootstrap it.
